name: Sierra Alpujata – circular from Fuengirola
region_intro: Touring cycling around Fuengirola offers a diverse landscape, blending coastal routes with challenging mountainous terrain. The region features well-maintained paths along the Mediterranean Sea, transitioning inland to mountain ranges such as the Sierra de Mijas and Montes de Málaga. These areas are characterized by pine forests, rocky outcrops, and significant elevation gains, alongside riverbeds and eucalyptus forests. Best touring cycling routes around Fuengirola The most popular touring cycling route is Sierra Alpujata – circular from Fuengirola, a difficult 32.2 miles (51.8 km) trail that takes 3 hours 55 minutes to complete. This route features substantial elevation gain through mountainous terrain. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Las Yucas Beach – Dolphin Show at Selwo Marina loop from Fuengirola, a moderate 16.9 miles (27.3 km) path. This route follows coastal paths and urban sections, offering views of the Mediterranean. Local touring cyclists also love the View from Cerro del Moro – Cerro del Moro loop from Fuengirola, a 28.3 miles (45.5 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain with high elevation gain, often completed in about 3 hours 26 minutes. Touring cycling around Fuengirola is defined by coastal promenades, mountain ranges, and pine forests. The network offers options for various ability levels, from leisurely coastal rides to challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Fuengirola are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 160 reviews. More than 2,300 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Fuengirola's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when touring cycling around Fuengirola?
A: Fuengirola offers a diverse cycling landscape. You'll find well-maintained coastal paths along the Mediterranean Sea for leisurely rides, such as those through Los Boliches and along the Paseo Marítimo. Inland, the terrain transitions to more challenging mountain ranges like the Sierra de Mijas and Montes de Málaga, featuring pine forests, rocky outcrops, and significant elevation gains. Riverbeds and eucalyptus forests also provide varied riding experiences.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easier ride?
A: Yes, Fuengirola offers routes for various ability levels. While many routes involve significant elevation, there are options for easier rides, particularly along the coastal promenades. Out of the 68 touring cycling routes available, 7 are rated as easy, and 16 are moderate, providing gentler options for those new to the area or seeking a less strenuous experience.

Q: What are some scenic highlights or landmarks I can see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer stunning views and pass by notable landmarks. You could cycle past the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/679625" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sohail Castle&lt;/a&gt;, which overlooks the coast. Other routes might take you near charming places like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/270791" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mijas Pueblo&lt;/a&gt;, a picturesque whitewashed village in the Sierra de Mijas, or offer views of the Mediterranean from various viewpoints. The coastal promenade itself is a scenic highlight, providing sea breezes and beautiful landscapes.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes in Fuengirola?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes around Fuengirola are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e968687595/sierra-alpujata-circular-from-fuengirola" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Alpujata – circular from Fuengirola&lt;/a&gt; is a popular option for experienced cyclists, offering a substantial elevation gain through mountainous terrain.

Q: What do other cyclists say about touring cycling in Fuengirola?
A: The touring cycling routes in Fuengirola are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 160 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, from coastal paths to challenging mountain ascents, and the stunning views of the Mediterranean and surrounding landscapes. Over 2,300 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore the area's varied routes.

Q: Are there any touring cycling routes that offer coastal views?
A: Absolutely. Fuengirola's extensive coastline means many routes incorporate the beautiful Mediterranean Sea. A great example is the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38216015" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Yucas Beach – Dolphin Show at Selwo Marina loop from Fuengirola&lt;/a&gt;, which follows coastal paths and urban sections, providing continuous views of the sea and opportunities to enjoy the sea breeze.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Fuengirola?
A: Fuengirola enjoys a pleasant climate, making it suitable for touring cycling for much of the year. The spring and autumn months generally offer the most comfortable temperatures for cycling, avoiding the peak heat of summer while still providing plenty of sunshine. Winter can also be a good option for those looking to escape colder climates, with mild temperatures ideal for outdoor activities.

Q: Are there any routes that take me through natural parks or forests?
A: Yes, several routes venture into the natural beauty surrounding Fuengirola. You can find paths that lead through pine forests and eucalyptus forests, offering a different kind of scenery and often a cooler, shadier ride. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31912717" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Eucalyptus Trail, Mijas – El Esparragal Natural Park loop from Fuengirola&lt;/a&gt; is an example of a route that takes you through these natural areas.

Q: Can I find challenging touring cycling routes with significant elevation gain?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Fuengirola offers numerous routes with substantial elevation gain. The inland mountain ranges, such as the Sierra de Mijas and Montes de Málaga, provide excellent opportunities for demanding climbs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31986851" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from Cerro del Moro – Cerro del Moro loop from Fuengirola&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, features over 1,000 meters of elevation gain, making it a difficult and rewarding ride.

Q: Are there any touring cycling routes that pass by historical sites or cultural attractions?
A: Beyond natural beauty, Fuengirola's routes can incorporate cultural and historical stops. The region is home to sites like the historic Sohail Castle and Roman archaeological sites. The charming whitewashed village of Mijas Pueblo, with its traditional streets and artisan shops, is also a frequent highlight on cycling routes, offering a glimpse into local culture.

Q: Are there options for family-friendly touring cycling in Fuengirola?
A: While many routes involve elevation, the flat coastal promenades provide excellent opportunities for family-friendly cycling. These paths are generally well-maintained and offer safe, scenic rides away from heavy traffic, perfect for a leisurely outing with children. Look for routes that stick to the beachfront areas for the easiest family experience.

Q: What kind of views can I expect on the more challenging mountain routes?
A: The more challenging mountain routes, particularly in the Sierra de Mijas, reward cyclists with panoramic views. You can expect breathtaking vistas of the Mediterranean Sea, the coastline stretching out below, and the rugged inland landscapes. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31921559" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Eagles' Viewpoint – El Esparragal Natural Park loop from Los Boliches&lt;/a&gt; are designed to showcase these spectacular viewpoints.
tips: Views of Sierra Alpujada and its peaks: Cerro del Aguila (1010 masl), Castillejos (1040 masl).

This balcony-viewpoint offers one of the most spectacular views of the province and the Gran Senda de Málaga. It is considered "the unique corner" of the Great Path - a "magical" place that not only offers beautiful views but also generates experiences and feelings forever. The Mediterranean Sea that we can see is surrounded by the Sierra de Mijas on the left and the Sierra de Alpujata on the right. In the valley is the Alaminos river.
